text.source_text: अर्भकमल्पम् ओको नीडम् , ‘ एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये ’ इति परिच्छिन्नायतनत्वात् , स्वशब्देन च ‘ अणीयान्व्रीहेर्वा यवाद्वा ’ इत्यणीयस्त्वव्यपदेशात् , शारीर एवाराग्रमात्रो जीव इहोपदिश्यते , न सर्वगतः परमात्मा — इति यदुक्तं तत्परिहर्तव्यम् । अत्रोच्यते — नायं दोषः । न ताव त्परिच्छिन्नदेशस्य सर्वगतत्वव्यपदेशः कथम प्युपपद्यते । सर्वगतस्य तु सर्वदेशेषु विद्यमानत्वात्परिच्छिन्नदेशव्यपदेशोऽपि कयाचिदपेक्षया सम्भवति । यथा सम स्तवसुधाधिपतिरपि हि सन् अयोध्याधिपतिरिति व्यपदिश्यते । कया पुनरपेक्षया सर्वगतः सन्नीश्वरोऽर्भकौका अणीयांश्च व्यपदिश्यत इति । निचाय्यत्वादेवमिति ब्रूमः । एवम् अणीयस्त्वादिगुणगणोपेत ईश्वरः , तत्र हृदयपुण्डरीके निचाय्यो द्रष्टव्य उपदिश्यते ; यथा सालग्रामे हरिः । तत्रास्य बुद्धिविज्ञानं ग्राहकम् । सर्वगतोऽपीश्वरस्तत्रोपास्यमानः प्रसीदति । व्योमवच्चैतद्द्रष्टव्यम् । यथा सर्वगतमपि सद्व्योम सूचीपाशाद्यपेक्षयार्भकौकोऽणीयश्च व्यपदिश्यते , एवं ब्रह्मापि । तदेवं निचाय्यत्वापेक्षं ब्रह्मणोऽर्भकौकस्त्वमणीयस्त्वं च , न पारमार्थिकम् । तत्र यदाशङ्क्यते — हृदयायतनत्वाद्ब्रह्मणो हृदयानां च प्रतिशरीरं भिन्नत्वाद्भिन्नायतनानां च शुकादीनामनेकत्वसावयवत्वानित्यत्वादिदोषदर्शनाद्ब्रह्मणोऽपि तत्प्रसङ्ग इति , तदपि परिहृतं भवति ॥ ७ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: arbhakamalpam oko nīḍam , ‘ eṣa ma ātmāntarhṛdaye ’ iti paricchinnāyatanatvāt , svaśabdena ca ‘ aṇīyānvrīhervā yavādvā ’ ityaṇīyastvavyapadeśāt , śārīra evārāgramātro jīva ihopadiśyate , na sarvagataḥ paramātmā — iti yaduktaṃ tatparihartavyam | atrocyate — nāyaṃ doṣaḥ | na tāva tparicchinnadeśasya sarvagatatvavyapadeśaḥ kathama pyupapadyate | sarvagatasya tu sarvadeśeṣu vidyamānatvātparicchinnadeśavyapadeśo'pi kayācidapekṣayā sambhavati | yathā sama stavasudhādhipatirapi hi san ayodhyādhipatiriti vyapadiśyate | kayā punarapekṣayā sarvagataḥ sannīśvaro'rbhakaukā aṇīyāṃśca vyapadiśyata iti | nicāyyatvādevamiti brūmaḥ | evam aṇīyastvādiguṇagaṇopeta īśvaraḥ , tatra hṛdayapuṇḍarīke nicāyyo draṣṭavya upadiśyate ; yathā sālagrāme hariḥ | tatrāsya buddhivijñānaṃ grāhakam | sarvagato'pīśvarastatropāsyamānaḥ prasīdati | vyomavaccaitaddraṣṭavyam | yathā sarvagatamapi sadvyoma sūcīpāśādyapekṣayārbhakauko'ṇīyaśca vyapadiśyate , evaṃ brahmāpi | tadevaṃ nicāyyatvāpekṣaṃ brahmaṇo'rbhakaukastvamaṇīyastvaṃ ca , na pāramārthikam | tatra yadāśaṅkyate — hṛdayāyatanatvādbrahmaṇo hṛdayānāṃ ca pratiśarīraṃ bhinnatvādbhinnāyatanānāṃ ca śukādīnāmanekatvasāvayavatvānityatvādidoṣadarśanādbrahmaṇo'pi tatprasaṅga iti , tadapi parihṛtaṃ bhavati || 7 ||

translation.literal_translation: The objection that should be dismissed is this: because of restricted locus ('this self within the heart') and because of smallness-expressions ('smaller than rice or barley'), it must be the embodied jiva, minute like a point, that is taught here, not the all-pervasive Supreme. Reply: this is no defect. For a truly spatially delimited thing, all-pervasiveness cannot fit; but for what is all-pervasive, designation in a limited place is possible under a specific standpoint, since it is present everywhere. Just as one who is lord of the whole earth is still called lord of Ayodhya under a perspective, so too. Under what perspective is the all-pervasive Lord called small-housed and minute? We say: because He is to be specifically focused upon. The Lord endowed with such qualities is taught as to be contemplated in the lotus of the heart, as Hari in a Salagrama. Here intellect-cognition functions as the grasping medium; and even though all-pervasive, when worshipped there the Lord becomes gracious. This should be understood like space: though all-pervasive, space is called tiny or minute with reference to a needle-eye or loop. Likewise Brahman. Thus Brahman's small-housedness and minuteness are relative to contemplative focusing, not ultimate. Therefore the further worry is also answered: if Brahman has heart as locus, and hearts differ per body, then like pots (many loci) Brahman would become many, composite, non-eternal, etc. That consequence does not follow.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.7 defuses alpatva-hrdaya objections by introducing nicayya-upasana relativity: local specification is pedagogical focus-mode, not ontological delimitation of Brahman.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Explains localizing expressions as contemplative focus-tools rather than metaphysical delimitation.
Objection-term answered by reclassifying smallness as pedagogical standpoint-language.
Interpreted as contemplative locus for practice, not ontic confinement of Brahman.
Allows practice-directed contextualization without altering ultimate referent.
Key rebuttal that apparent spatial restriction does not genuinely bound Brahman.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit upadhi/vyavahara terminology and atta-grahana technical choices in records 1.2.016-1.2.022.
